title: microchilus hirtellus
intro: Microchilus hirtellus, also known as the hairy milk vine, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. It is characterized by its hairy stems and leaves, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Microchilus hirtellus is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be used to make a tincture or salve, which are often used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
